prerequisites: A Thinkific site whose plan and account permit Admin API access (access tokens are granted on behalf of the site by the Site Owner).
Your Thinkific site subdomain, used as the X-Auth-Subdomain value (the configured X-Auth-Subdomain config field).
An API key generated for your site, used as the api_key secret.
Network access to the Thinkific Admin API at https://api.thinkific.com/api/public/v1.

authMethods.name: API Key (X-Auth headers)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Thinkific API key. The connector's api_key secret is sent as the X-Auth-API-Key header and the X-Auth-Subdomain config field as the X-Auth-Subdomain header, identifying your site. Thinkific documents this as API Key / API Access Token authorization, granted on behalf of the Site Owner. This is the only method this connector supports (OAuth is not used).

setupSteps.title: Confirm Admin API access for your site
setupSteps.body: Thinkific grants Admin API access tokens on behalf of the site (Site Owner). Make sure your Thinkific site and account permit API access before configuring the connector.

setupSteps.title: Find your site subdomain
setupSteps.body: Determine your Thinkific site subdomain (the host portion of your Thinkific URL). Enter it in the X-Auth-Subdomain config field. It is sent as the X-Auth-Subdomain request header to identify your site.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: In your Thinkific admin settings, generate an API key for the site. Copy the key and provide it as the api_key secret. The connector sends it as the X-Auth-API-Key header on every request to the Admin API.

setupSteps.title: Configure and run the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the api_key secret and the X-Auth-Subdomain value. The connector calls the Thinkific Admin API (base address https://api.thinkific.com/api/public/v1) to read course data.

sources.title: Thinkific Admin API documentation (authorization and base address)
